zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John de Pêche II於大約1280年出生在英國瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓時，他的父親John Pêche26歲，母親Margaret Moyne32歲。他於1321年與Alice Hayward在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1340年在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John de Pêche II于大约1280年出生在英国瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓时，他的父亲John Pêche26岁，母亲Margaret Moyne32岁。他于1321年与Alice Hayward在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1340年在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾喬姆利頓享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Pêche
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a variant of   Pech   ‘pitch, tar’.
Americanized form of Slovenian Peče or of its Germanized form Petsche , which is also a Gottscheerish (i.e. Gottschee German) surname.

variants.name: Pech
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Carlos, Jose, Manuel, Alvaro, Diego, Edelmira, Emilio, Francisco, Gilberto, Jorge, Jose Angel.  German Gerhard, Hans, Ilse, Lutz, Manfred, Wolf.

Czech and Slovak; Sorbian (Pěch and P'ech): from a pet form of the personal name Petr (Czech), Pětš (Lower Sorbian), and Peter (Slovak). This is also a Germanized form of the Upper Sorbian cognate Pjech.

German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German pech (German Pech) ‘pitch, tar’, presumably a nickname for someone with exceptionally dark skin or hair, or a metonymic occupational name for someone who prepared, sold, or used pitch. This may in part also be the origin of a homonymic Polish, Czech, and Slovak surname (compare above and below).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John de Pêche II was born about 1280, in Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England, his father, John Pêche, was 26 and his mother, Margaret Moyne, was 32. He married Alice Hayward in 1321, in Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. He died in 1340, in Wormleighton, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 61.

Il a épousé Alice Hayward en 1321, à Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. ko.lifeSummary: Sir John de Pêche II(이)가 대략 1280년 영국 워윅실르 워레이턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Pêche(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Moyne(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1321년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르에서 Alice Hayward(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워레이턴에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.
